challenges.name: Brutus
challenges.keyTakeaway: Linux auth.log parsing — SSH brute-force detection, identifying successful logins, and tracking post-exploitation activity from authentication logs

challenges.name: Unit42
challenges.keyTakeaway: Sysmon log analysis — filtering process creation events, network connections, and registry modifications to trace malware execution on Windows

challenges.name: Campfire-1
challenges.keyTakeaway: Kerberoasting detection — identifying suspicious Kerberos TGS requests (Event ID 4769) with RC4 encryption in Domain Controller security logs

challenges.name: Logjammer
challenges.keyTakeaway: Multi-source Windows event log analysis — correlating Security, System, Defender, Firewall, and PowerShell logs to reconstruct an attack chain

challenges.name: Campfire-2
challenges.keyTakeaway: AS-REP Roasting detection — filtering Kerberos authentication events (Event ID 4768) to identify attacks against accounts without preauthentication

challenges.name: CrownJewel-1
challenges.keyTakeaway: NTDS.dit dump detection — correlating Windows event logs with MFT analysis to identify Volume Shadow Copy abuse on a Domain Controller

challenges.name: Bumblebee
challenges.keyTakeaway: Web application log forensics — correlating NGINX access logs with SQLite database records to trace credential theft and unauthorized access

challenges.name: Tick Tock
challenges.keyTakeaway: Remote access and C2 detection — analyzing TeamViewer artifacts, Merlin C2 agent traces, and system time manipulation through event logs

challenges.name: Heartbreaker
challenges.keyTakeaway: Multi-artifact phishing investigation — correlating email artifacts, Sysmon logs, browser history, and prefetch files to trace a full attack chain

challenges.name: Tracer
challenges.keyTakeaway: PsExec lateral movement detection — identifying service installation, named pipe creation, and execution artifacts across prefetch files and event logs

challenges.name: Nubilum-1
challenges.keyTakeaway: Cloud log forensics — analyzing AWS CloudTrail logs with jq to trace credential compromise, resource creation, and C2 server deployment

challenges.name: i-like-to
challenges.keyTakeaway: Multi-artifact CVE investigation — correlating MFT data, event logs, SQL dumps, and PowerShell history to reconstruct a MOVEit Transfer exploitation
